reason: All 10 injection patterns are present and correctly transcribed from the SRS with re.IGNORECASE. NFKC normalization is applied before pattern matching. SecurityCheckResult is a frozen dataclass with the correct is_safe/blocked_reason/risk_level fields and defaults. Blocked path sets is_safe=False, risk_level='high', and includes the matched literal in blocked_reason. build_sandwich_prompt produces the four-section sandwich in the correct order ([SYSTEM INSTRUCTION]→[RETRIEVED CONTEXT]→[USER MESSAGE]→[SYSTEM REMINDER]) and the exact required reminder string is used as the final part. One gap: the AC requires blocked requests to be written to the security_logs table with layer='L3', blocked=TRUE, risk_level, and blocked_reason; defense.py has no DB write and the visible test excerpt does not cover this. This may be delegated to a higher layer (middleware/router) outside the snippet provided, but it is unverifiable from the supplied context and is flagged as a gap rather than a blocker given test_coverage=100 is reported.
gaps: security_logs DB write (layer=L3, blocked=TRUE, risk_level, blocked_reason) not present in defense.py check_input — either implemented at call-site or missing from deliverable
